purpose: First normalized candidate layer for Eye of Azshara boss loot. This file intentionally does not claim that all Wowhead boss-page drop rows are real boss-specific loot. It points to the compact source CSVs, defines the classification fields, and records the first-pass rules needed to build a full row-level normalized output.
important_note: Do not use this file yet as final boss loot truth. Boss-page drop tables contain a mixture of likely boss drops, zone drops, Timewalking rows, quest/currency/profession rows, event rows, global rows and unknown rows. Unknown values are allowed and should not be invented.

source_files.notes: The Parjesh drops file was the first extraction and is marked tiny/partial in the discovery summary. It should be regenerated as compact CSV before final row-level normalization.
Boss page and compact CSV are available. Rows still need classification because the table includes non-boss-loot noise.
Boss page and compact CSV are available. Includes drops, currencies and skinning/source noise that must be separated.
Boss page and compact CSV are available. Includes zone/global/event/noisy rows that must be separated.
Boss page and compact CSV are available. Final boss has additional objective/currency noise and unlock context.

first_pass_classification_rules.description: Rows with Wowhead item class 4 are armor/accessory gear candidates. These may still be zone drops or Timewalking rows and are not automatically boss-specific.
Rows with Wowhead item class 3 in Legion dungeon data are often relic/artifact related and should be separated from normal gear upgrade logic until artifact relevance is handled.
Rows with Wowhead item class 12 in the current extracts appear to include quest-like/objective/currency-style rows. Do not treat as gear drops.
Rows with Wowhead item class 9 are recipe/profession related and should not be counted as boss gear until separately verified.
Rows with Wowhead item class 15 are miscellaneous and may include event boxes, profession materials, Remix/Timewalking artifacts or other non-loot-system rows.
Rows with names starting with Timeless are likely Timewalking/temporary utility rows and should not be treated as standard Legion boss gear.
Rows with names starting with Memento of Epoch are likely event/Timewalking/Remix-style rows and should be separated from standard boss loot.
Rows containing Artifactium should be treated as event/Timewalking/Remix-style currency/material until confirmed.
Rare/epic equipment rows with normal Legion dungeon item levels are possible gear candidates, but still need source verification to determine boss_drop vs zone_drop.

classification_notes.source_raw: The compact CSVs preserve a source-like numeric field from the Wowhead extraction. This field should be decoded in the next pass before making strong boss_drop/zone_drop claims.
classification_notes.boss_drop_vs_zone_drop: Rows on a boss page can still show Zone Drop in the original Wowhead table. Candidate rows should not be recommended as boss-specific until the source text or Blizzard API confirms it.

difficulty_modes.1: Normal or normal-like context in current extract
difficulty_modes.2: Heroic or heroic-like context in current extract
difficulty_modes.8: Mythic+ or derived current-context value in Wowhead table
difficulty_modes.23: Mythic Dungeon or related current-context value in Wowhead table
difficulty_modes.24: Timewalking or timewalking encounter journal context in current extract

classification_notes.mode_mapping_status: provisional; verify against Wowhead/Blizzard API before final use

next_actions.description: Create a compact CSV for Warlord Parjesh matching the other four bosses, because the current Parjesh drops file is an early tiny JSON extraction.

Create a small script or Lovable generator that reads the compact CSV files and writes a full row-level boss-loot-candidate.generated.json using the candidate_row_schema and first_pass_classification_rules.
Decode or preserve the Wowhead source_raw values so we can distinguish boss drops, zone drops and other source types more reliably.
Compare candidate boss loot against Blizzard Game Data API / encounter journal / item metadata where available.
Only after boss loot is cleaner, compare gear candidates against known group character snapshots to answer which dungeon/boss is most worth doing.
